idea-plugin.name: DTA - Development Tools for Android
idea-plugin.description:
Give your AI coding assistant direct access to Android devices. DTA connects AI agents to your running app through the Model Context Protocol — they can see the screen, read network traffic, inspect the layout tree, mock responses, and interact with the UI.

Features

Layout Inspector — Full UI hierarchy with Compose support (composable names, recomposition counts), WebView DOM trees, and Chrome Custom Tab content. Device screenshot with hover highlighting and click-to-select. Bidirectional selection sync between the tree, screenshot, and MCP tools.

Network Inspector — Auto-captures HTTP traffic (OkHttp), Chrome Custom Tab requests, and WebView network activity from the moment the app launches. Request/response headers, bodies, and timing.

WebSocket Inspector — Live WebSocket connections with sent/received messages and connection lifecycle.

MCP Server — Built-in HTTP MCP server for connecting AI agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Android Studio Gemini, Claude Desktop, VS Code Copilot). Pick your agent from the dropdown, copy the config snippet, paste — done.

Auto-Inject Sidekick — No changes to your project files. The plugin injects the dta-sidekick inspection library into debug builds automatically via a Gradle init script.

Build & Launch — The run_app MCP tool builds, installs, and launches your app with sidekick injected — your AI assistant can go from source code to a running, inspectable app in a single tool call.

How It Works

  1. Install the plugin and open any Android project
  2. Run your app on a device or emulator (debug build)
  3. Open the DTA Inspector tool window — select your device and app
  4. Browse layout, network, and WebSocket data in the inspector tabs
  5. Connect your AI agent via the MCP tab for AI-powered debugging

Links

  • Video overview
  • Article: Debugging Android Apps with AI
  • GitHub
